title: fix(core): pass agent workspace into converted workspace tools

similarOrDuplicatePRs.title: fix(mastracode): subagents inherit workspace from parent agent
similarOrDuplicatePRs.relationship: related but different â€” 13940 fixes subagent workspace propagation, this PR fixes workspace fallback in listWorkspaceTools ToolOptions

relatedIssues.title: [BUG] Built-in workspace tools can throw NO_WORKSPACE despite configured agent workspace

summary: Fixes a bug where built-in workspace tools (write_file, execute_command, etc.) throw WorkspaceNotAvailableError even when the agent has a workspace configured. The root cause is that `Agent.listWorkspaceTools` resolves the workspace but does not pass it into the `ToolOptions` object when converting tools via `makeCoreTool`. Since `makeCoreTool` builds context as `execOptions.workspace ?? options.workspace`, paths without exec-time workspace fail. The fix is a single-line addition of `workspace` to the options object at the conversion site. Includes a well-written regression test and a changeset.
maintainerNotes: High-quality, minimal fix. The actual code change is a single line (`workspace,` added to ToolOptions in `listWorkspaceTools`). The bug is real and well-documented in issue #13806 with clear reproduction steps. The regression test directly exercises the failing path by calling `listWorkspaceTools` and executing the converted write_file tool without exec-time workspace. CI checks are pending (not failed) â€” the core tests, E2E, and other test suites haven't run yet (likely needs secrets/authorization). The Vercel deploy failures are expected for external contributors (authorization required). Author notes that `pnpm typecheck` fails on unrelated issues in the checkout. This is not superseded by #13940 which addresses a different workspace propagation path (subagents).
